_comment: ShieldLab - master bibliography. Every numeric value in the other data files carries a 'ref' key pointing here. Edit/add entries freely; keys are cited in the UI and in exported reports.

NCRP147.citation: NCRP Report No. 147, 'Structural Shielding Design for Medical X-Ray Imaging Facilities', National Council on Radiation Protection and Measurements, Bethesda, MD (2004).
NCRP147.role: Core methodology and data for diagnostic X-ray, fluoroscopy, mammography, CT and dental shielding: design goals, occupancy factors, workload distributions, unshielded kermas, transmission (Archer) parameters.

NCRP151.citation: NCRP Report No. 151, 'Structural Shielding Design and Evaluation for Megavoltage X- and Gamma-Ray Radiotherapy Facilities', National Council on Radiation Protection and Measurements, Bethesda, MD (2005).
NCRP151.role: Megavoltage (LINAC) vault methodology: B = P d^2 / (W U T), 0.1 % head leakage, IMRT factor, time-averaged dose rate checks. Values cross-checked against IAEA SRS 47.
NCRP151.note: Report not freely distributable; methodology and equations encoded from the report formalism as also published in IAEA SRS 47 and peer-reviewed summaries.

NCRP172.citation: NCRP Report No. 172, 'Reference Levels and Achievable Doses in Medical and Dental Imaging: Recommendations for the United States', NCRP, Bethesda, MD (2012).
NCRP172.role: Context for realistic per-patient dose and workload defaults.

SRS47.citation: IAEA Safety Reports Series No. 47, 'Radiation Protection in the Design of Radiotherapy Facilities', STI/PUB/1223, International Atomic Energy Agency, Vienna (2006).
SRS47.role: LINAC vault data: TVL1/TVLe for concrete, steel and lead at 4-18 MV (primary and leakage), patient scatter fractions vs angle, maze and door guidance, Co-60 and HDR data.

GSR_PART3.citation: IAEA Safety Standards Series No. GSR Part 3, 'Radiation Protection and Safety of Radiation Sources: International Basic Safety Standards', IAEA, Vienna (2014), Schedule III.
GSR_PART3.role: International dose limits: occupational effective dose 20 mSv/y averaged over 5 consecutive years (max 50 mSv in any single year); public 1 mSv/y.

ICRP103.citation: ICRP Publication 103, 'The 2007 Recommendations of the International Commission on Radiological Protection', Ann. ICRP 37 (2-4), 2007.
ICRP103.role: Underlying system of radiological protection: justification, optimisation, dose limitation.

NRRC_R01.citation: NRRC-R-01, Technical Regulation 'Radiation Safety', Nuclear and Radiological Regulatory Commission (NRRC), Kingdom of Saudi Arabia (2022).
NRRC_R01.role: Saudi dose limits - Section 10: clause 22 occupational (20 mSv/y avg 5 y, 50 mSv max single year; lens 20 mSv/y; extremities/skin 500 mSv/y; apprentices 16-18 y: 6 mSv/y), clause 23 pregnant/breastfeeding worker 1 mSv to embryo/fetus, clause 24 public (1 mSv/y effective; lens 15 mSv/y; skin 50 mSv/y). Section 8 clause 20: dose constraints are practice-specific, set below NRRC generic values (no fixed numeric design constraint given in R-01).

KSA_LAW.citation: Law of Nuclear and Radiological Control, Royal Decree No. M/81 of 25/7/1439 AH (11 April 2018), Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.
KSA_LAW.role: Legal basis of the Saudi regulatory framework (NRRC).

SFDA_NDRL.citation: SFDA MDS-G-008-V2, 'National Diagnostic Reference Levels', Version 2.0, Saudi Food and Drug Authority (26/10/2022).
SFDA_NDRL.role: Saudi national DRLs (CT DLP values, etc.) used for KSA-specific default workloads.

RG839.citation: U.S. NRC Regulatory Guide 8.39, 'Release of Patients Administered Radioactive Materials', U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (April 1997).
RG839.role: I-131 patient-as-source biokinetics: occupancy factor E, uptake fractions and effective half-lives (Table B-1), dose-integration formalism (Equations B-2/B-3).

RPII09.citation: RPII 09/01, 'The Design of Diagnostic Medical Facilities where Ionising Radiation is used - A Code of Practice', Radiological Protection Institute of Ireland, Dublin (June 2009).
RPII09.role: European constraint-style design basis (0.3 mSv/y public dose constraint) and room design good practice.

BIR2012.citation: Sutton, D.G., Martin, C.J., Williams, J.R., Peet, D.J., 'Radiation Shielding for Diagnostic Radiology', 2nd ed., British Institute of Radiology, London (2012) - Chapter 2 excerpt provided by user.
BIR2012.role: Barrier-height and tertiary (ceiling/wall) scatter methodology: scatter air kerma per patient KAP (interventional) or per DLP (CT) as a function of barrier height and ceiling height.

TG108.citation: Madsen, M.T., Anderson, J.A., Halama, J.R., Kleck, J., Simpkin, D.J., Votaw, J.R., Wendt, R.E. III, Williams, L.E., Yester, M.V., 'AAPM Task Group 108: PET and PET/CT Shielding Requirements', Medical Physics 33(1):4-15 (2006).
TG108.role: PET/CT shielding methodology: patient-as-source with uptake/imaging phases, decay and patient self-attenuation factors, broad-beam transmission for 511 keV photons in lead, steel and concrete.

OUMANO2025.citation: Oumano, M., Wendt, R., Botti, J., et al., 'Shielding resources for four common radiopharmaceuticals utilized for imaging and therapy: Tc-99m, F-18, I-131, and Lu-177', Journal of Applied Clinical Medical Physics 26(5):e70084 (2025). doi:10.1002/acm2.70084.
OUMANO2025.role: Monte-Carlo-derived (GATE 9.2 / MCNP6) broad-beam Archer transmission fits and HVL/TVL for Tc-99m, F-18, I-131 and Lu-177 in lead and concrete; dose-rate constants.

XCOM.citation: Berger, M.J., Hubbell, J.H., Seltzer, S.M., et al., 'XCOM: Photon Cross Sections Database', NIST Standard Reference Database 8 (XGAM), NBSIR 87-3597, National Institute of Standards and Technology.
XCOM.role: Mass attenuation coefficients (mu/rho) for any element, compound or mixture, 1 keV - 100 GeV; basis of the generic-material engine.

HUBBELL.citation: Hubbell, J.H., Seltzer, S.M., 'Tables of X-Ray Mass Attenuation Coefficients and Mass Energy-Absorption Coefficients 1 keV to 20 MeV for Elements Z = 1 to 92 and 48 Additional Substances of Dosimetric Interest', NISTIR 5632, NIST (2004 web version).
HUBBELL.role: Tabulated mu/rho grids used for the built-in material library.

ANS643.citation: ANSI/ANS-6.4.3-1991, 'Gamma-Ray Attenuation Coefficients and Buildup Factors for Engineering Materials', American Nuclear Society, La Grange Park, IL (1991).
ANS643.role: Point-isotropic buildup factors (Geometric-Progression fitting form) for the generic broad-beam model where no measured Archer/TVL fit exists.

ARCHER1983.citation: Archer, B.R., Thornby, J.I., Bushong, S.C., 'Diagnostic X-ray shielding design based on an empirical model of photon attenuation', Health Physics 44(5):507-517 (1983).
ARCHER1983.role: Origin of the three-parameter (alpha, beta, gamma) broad-beam transmission model used throughout.

SIMPKIN1995.citation: Simpkin, D.J., 'Transmission data for shielding diagnostic x-ray facilities', Health Physics 68(5):704-709 (1995).
SIMPKIN1995.role: Fitted Archer parameters for broad diagnostic beams in lead, concrete, gypsum, steel, plate glass and wood (basis of the NCRP 147 transmission data).

CFR20.citation: U.S. Code of Federal Regulations, 10 CFR 20.1301, 'Dose limits for individual members of the public'.
CFR20.role: Origin of the 0.02 mSv 'in any one hour' criterion used as the LINAC time-averaged dose-rate check (per NCRP 151).
